text:
RAPID CITY, S.D. (KOTA) - Early on Friday, Sept. 13. law enforcement officers from multiple departments gathered at Jerry’s Donuts in Rapid City for their AnnualCops on Donut Shopsevent.

The event raised money for Special Olympics South Dakota in partnership with Jerry’s Donuts and South Dakota Law Enforcement Torch Run.

Any donation amount was accepted and with each donation came a free donut.

“Cops on Donut Shops probably started with somebody sitting around a table and said, ’We oughtta put a cop on a donut shop and use it as a fundraiser,’” said the Director of South Dakota Law Enforcement Torch Run, Dave Stratton.

Fundraisers hoped to raise more money than Sioux Falls to win the friendly fundraising competition. While the event is over, Special Olympics South Dakota is always acceptingdonationsto support athletes.

title: Cops on Donut Shops to raise money for Special Olympics
